title: Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good
excerpt: "I don't think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation," one expert told TechCrunch.
content_html:
White House science advisor Michael Kratsios said that Moonshot, the Chinese company behind Kimi K3, the largest available open-weight LLM, built its model by copying Anthropic’s Fable LLM while using chips that aren’t cleared for export to China.

“Large-scale, covert industrial distillation aimed at stealing proprietary U.S. technology and undermining American research is unacceptable,” Kratsios wrote, amid reported discussions about banning Chinese open-weight models that have roiled the AI sector. Moonshot did not respond to questions about its training process, and Kratsios did not share more details about the sources of his allegations.

Kratsios’ tweet echoed comments from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent that “we are finding watermarks of our U.S. large language models on many of the Chinese models, and that that’s unacceptable.” It’s not clear what those watermarks consist of, and the Treasury Department did not respond to a query.

However, experts are skeptical that distillation — the process of querying an LLM to determine its inner workings and copy its capabilities — is responsible for the advanced capabilities that Kimi K3 displays.

“I don’t think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation,” Braden Hancock, a researcher at the Laude Institute and co-founder of Snorkel AI, told TechCrunch. “There’s just not even frankly time, right? Fable’s only been publicly available since July 1st. You can’t distill that much data, train a model, and release it in two weeks.”

“I’ve been of the opinion that distillation is becoming less and less impactful over time as the Chinese models get closer to the frontier and the training regime shifts to [reinforcement learning],” Nathan Lambert, an AI researcher at the Allen Institute for AI, said in a podcast released yesterday. “[I]f it were the case, everyone would be easily able to catch up to a GLM or to a K3 by using its data for distillation. But we have not, or we won’t see this, from supervised fine-tuning alone.”

Performing distillation requires a lab to systematically query its target model in order to generate data that can be used for post-training. Sometimes this explicitly involves asking the model to articulate its chain-of-thought to understand how it solves problems. Other times, the prompts and responses from a model are used to train a new model in a process called supervised fine-tuning, or SFT.

It’s this fine-tuning process that can result in a model ostensibly created by a third party claiming that it is Claude. Fine tuning is where, in Lambert’s view, the “model picks up its manners.”

But Lambert says that the benefits of SFT are becoming less important as models become more complex. To distill Fable-like capabilities would likely require reinforcement learning techniques. In many cases, that means having an agent of the larger model grade the smaller model’s responses, and adjusting based on the grade.

The more advanced techniques also require more significant infrastructure. Large reinforcement learning runs can require tens of millions of agents. Using a frontier lab’s API to do that “would be insanely expensive and potentially it would probably be a time bottleneck because these models are pretty slow and to be frank might not even give you a performance uplift.”

It seems likely that previous frontier models might have contributed to Kimi; Anthropic publicly accused Moonshot, DeepSeek, and MiniMax of systematically distilling its models earlier this year. Anthropic said it discovered millions of exchanges between its models and users it identified at those companies through IP addresses and other meta data. Those queries were “distinct from normal usage patterns, reflecting deliberate capability extraction rather than legitimate use.” Anthropic didn’t respond to TechCrunch’s queries about Fable distillation.

However, distillation is seen as common among AI companies, not just in China. Elon Musk testified earlier this year that his company SpaceXAI distilled OpenAI models to develop Grok, and that the practice was common in the industry. The line between distillation and developing synthetic datasets, for example, can be fairly blurry.

“[I]n general, Americans are understating the technical expertise of these Chinese teams,” Hancock said. “One of the founders of Moonshot was a CMU PhD student. These are legitimate researchers and engineers doing solid work. …if American models ground to a halt, I think China’s progress would slow, but would still continue. They’re not just riding coattails here.”

It’s also hard to disentangle distillation from the second part of Kratsios’ comment — that Moonshot had obtained advanced Nvidia Chips, Grace Blackwell 300s, and also accessed GB300 equipped-servers in Thailand. Those chips are banned from export to China, but a black market exists, according to Sam Bresnick, a research fellow at Georgetown’s Center for Security and Emerging Technology. In May, the founder of Supermicro, a U.S. server builder, was indicted for smuggling advanced chips into China.

“I am a proponent of know your customer laws for data centers across the world,” Bresnick said. “If you are letting a company conduct huge training runs on your state-of-the-art hardware, there needs to be a reporting mechanism for who that company is and what they’re doing.”

President Joe Biden’s Department of Commerce proposed federal know-your-customer rules for data centers in 2024, but no further progress appears to have been made under Donald Trump. Exporters shipping advanced chips abroad, however, are supposed to ensure they are only used for approved purposes.

When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.

Tim Fernholz is a journalist who writes about technology, finance and public policy. He has closely covered the rise of the private space industry and is the author of Rocket Billionaires: Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos and the New Space Race. Formerly, he was a senior reporter at Quartz, the global business news site, for more than a decade, and began his career as a political reporter in Washington, D.C. You can contact or verify outreach from Tim by emailing tim.fernholz@techcrunch.com or via an encrypted message to tim_fernholz.21 on Signal.

blocks.html_en: White House science advisor Michael Kratsios said that Moonshot, the Chinese company behind Kimi K3, the largest available open-weight LLM, built its model by copying Anthropic’s Fable LLM while using chips that aren’t cleared for export to China.

sentences.en: White House science advisor Michael Kratsios said that Moonshot, the Chinese company behind Kimi K3, the largest available open-weight LLM, built its model by copying Anthropic’s Fable LLM while using chips that aren’t cleared for export to China.
sentences.zh: 白宫科学顾问 Michael Kratsios 表示，Moonshot 是最大的开放式法学硕士 Kimi K3 背后的中国公司，它通过复制 Anthropic 的 Fable LLM 建立模型，同时使用未获准出口到中国的芯片。

blocks.html_en: “Large-scale, covert industrial distillation aimed at stealing proprietary U.S. technology and undermining American research is unacceptable,” Kratsios wrote, amid reported discussions about banning Chinese open-weight models that have roiled the AI sector. Moonshot did not respond to questions about its training process, and Kratsios did not share more details about the sources of his allegations.

sentences.en: “Large-scale, covert industrial distillation aimed at stealing proprietary U.S. technology and undermining American research is unacceptable,” Kratsios wrote, amid reported discussions about banning Chinese open-weight models that have roiled the AI sector.
sentences.zh: Kratsios 写道：“旨在窃取美国专有技术和破坏美国研究的大规模、隐蔽的工业蒸馏是不可接受的。”据报道，正在讨论禁止中国开放权重模型，这些模型扰乱了人工智能行业。

sentences.en: Moonshot did not respond to questions about its training process, and Kratsios did not share more details about the sources of his allegations.
sentences.zh: Moonshot 没有回应有关其培训过程的问题，Kratsios 也没有透露有关其指控来源的更多细节。

blocks.html_en: Kratsios’ tweet echoed comments from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent that “we are finding watermarks of our U.S. large language models on many of the Chinese models, and that that’s unacceptable.” It’s not clear what those watermarks consist of, and the Treasury Department did not respond to a query.

sentences.en: Kratsios’ tweet echoed comments from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent that “we are finding watermarks of our U.S. large language models on many of the Chinese models, and that that’s unacceptable.”
sentences.zh: Kratsios 的推文呼应了财政部长 Scott Bessent 的评论，即“我们在许多中国模型上发现了美国大语言模型的水印，这是不可接受的。”

sentences.en: It’s not clear what those watermarks consist of, and the Treasury Department did not respond to a query.
sentences.zh: 目前尚不清楚这些水印由什么组成，财政部也没有回应询问。

blocks.html_en: However, experts are skeptical that distillation — the process of querying an LLM to determine its inner workings and copy its capabilities — is responsible for the advanced capabilities that Kimi K3 displays.

sentences.en: However, experts are skeptical that distillation — the process of querying an LLM to determine its inner workings and copy its capabilities — is responsible for the advanced capabilities that Kimi K3 displays.
sentences.zh: 然而，专家们对蒸馏（查询法学硕士以确定其内部运作并复制其功能的过程）是否是 Kimi K3 所展示的高级功能的原因表示怀疑。

blocks.html_en: “I don’t think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation,” Braden Hancock, a researcher at the Laude Institute and co-founder of Snorkel AI, told TechCrunch. “There’s just not even frankly time, right? Fable’s only been publicly available since July 1st. You can’t distill that much data, train a model, and release it in two weeks.”

sentences.en: “I don’t think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation,” Braden Hancock, a researcher at the Laude Institute and co-founder of Snorkel AI, told TechCrunch.
sentences.zh: 劳德研究所 (Laude Institute) 研究员、Snorkel AI 联合创始人布雷登·汉考克 (Braden Hancock) 告诉 TechCrunch：“我认为，在《神鬼寓言》严格蒸馏之后，你不会得到如此强大、如此迅速的模型。”

sentences.en: “There’s just not even frankly time, right?
sentences.zh: “坦白说，根本就没有时间，对吧？

sentences.en: Fable’s only been publicly available since July 1st.
sentences.zh: 《神鬼寓言》自 7 月 1 日起才向公众开放。

sentences.en: You can’t distill that much data, train a model, and release it in two weeks.”
sentences.zh: 你不可能在两周内提取那么多数据、训练模型并发布它。”

blocks.html_en: “I’ve been of the opinion that distillation is becoming less and less impactful over time as the Chinese models get closer to the frontier and the training regime shifts to [reinforcement learning],” Nathan Lambert, an AI researcher at the Allen Institute for AI, said in a podcast released yesterday. “[I]f it were the case, everyone would be easily able to catch up to a GLM or to a K3 by using its data for distillation. But we have not, or we won’t see this, from supervised fine-tuning alone.”

sentences.en: “I’ve been of the opinion that distillation is becoming less and less impactful over time as the Chinese models get closer to the frontier and the training regime shifts to [reinforcement learning],” Nathan Lambert, an AI researcher at the Allen Institute for AI, said in a podcast released yesterday.
sentences.zh: 艾伦人工智能研究所的人工智能研究员内森·兰伯特 (Nathan Lambert) 在昨天发布的播客中表示：“我一直认为，随着时间的推移，随着中国模型越来越接近前沿，并且训练制度转向[强化学习]，蒸馏的影响力越来越小。”

sentences.en: “[I]f it were the case, everyone would be easily able to catch up to a GLM or to a K3 by using its data for distillation.
sentences.zh: “如果是这样的话，每个人都可以通过使用其数据进行蒸馏来轻松赶上 GLM 或 K3。

sentences.en: But we have not, or we won’t see this, from supervised fine-tuning alone.”
sentences.zh: 但我们还没有，或者说我们不会仅仅通过监督微调来看到这一点。”

Sometimes this explicitly involves asking the model to articulate its chain-of-thought to understand how it solves problems. Other times, the prompts and responses from a model are used to train a new model in a process called supervised fine-tuning, or SFT.

sentences.en: Performing distillation requires a lab to systematically query its target model in order to generate data that can be used for post-training.
sentences.zh: 执行蒸馏需要实验室系统地查询其目标模型，以便生成可用于后期训练的数据。

sentences.en: Sometimes this explicitly involves asking the model to articulate its chain-of-thought to understand how it solves problems.
sentences.zh: 有时，这明确涉及要求模型阐明其思想链以了解其如何解决问题。

sentences.en: Other times, the prompts and responses from a model are used to train a new model in a process called supervised fine-tuning, or SFT.
sentences.zh: 其他时候，模型的提示和响应用于在称为监督微调（SFT）的过程中训练新模型。

blocks.html_en: It’s this fine-tuning process that can result in a model ostensibly created by a third party claiming that it is Claude. Fine tuning is where, in Lambert’s view, the “model picks up its manners.”

sentences.en: It’s this fine-tuning process that can result in a model ostensibly created by a third party claiming that it is Claude.
sentences.zh: 正是这种微调过程可能会导致表面上由第三方创建的模型声称是克劳德。

sentences.en: Fine tuning is where, in Lambert’s view, the “model picks up its manners.”
sentences.zh: 在兰伯特看来，微调是“模型恢复其行为方式”的地方。

blocks.html_en: But Lambert says that the benefits of SFT are becoming less important as models become more complex. To distill Fable-like capabilities would likely require reinforcement learning techniques. In many cases, that means having an agent of the larger model grade the smaller model’s responses, and adjusting based on the grade.

sentences.en: But Lambert says that the benefits of SFT are becoming less important as models become more complex.
sentences.zh: 但 Lambert 表示，随着模型变得越来越复杂，SFT 的好处变得越来越不重要。

sentences.en: To distill Fable-like capabilities would likely require reinforcement learning techniques.
sentences.zh: 要提炼类似寓言的能力可能需要强化学习技术。

sentences.en: In many cases, that means having an agent of the larger model grade the smaller model’s responses, and adjusting based on the grade.
sentences.zh: 在许多情况下，这意味着让较大模型的代理对较小模型的响应进行评分，并根据评分进行调整。

blocks.html_en: The more advanced techniques also require more significant infrastructure. Large reinforcement learning runs can require tens of millions of agents. Using a frontier lab’s API to do that “would be insanely expensive and potentially it would probably be a time bottleneck because these models are pretty slow and to be frank might not even give you a performance uplift.”

sentences.zh: 更先进的技术也需要更重要的基础设施。

sentences.en: Large reinforcement learning runs can require tens of millions of agents.
sentences.zh: 大型强化学习运行可能需要数千万个代理。

sentences.en: Using a frontier lab’s API to do that “would be insanely expensive and potentially it would probably be a time bottleneck because these models are pretty slow and to be frank might not even give you a performance uplift.”
sentences.zh: 使用前沿实验室的 API 来做到这一点“将非常昂贵，并且可能会成为时间瓶颈，因为这些模型非常慢，坦率地说，甚至可能不会给你带来性能提升。”

sentences.zh: 似乎以前的前沿模型可能对 Kimi 做出了贡献； Anthropic 今年早些时候公开指责 Moonshot、DeepSeek 和 MiniMax 系统地提炼其模型。

sentences.en: Anthropic said it discovered millions of exchanges between its models and users it identified at those companies through IP addresses and other meta data.

sentences.en: Those queries were “distinct from normal usage patterns, reflecting deliberate capability extraction rather than legitimate use.”

sentences.en: Anthropic didn’t respond to TechCrunch’s queries about Fable distillation.

Exporters shipping advanced chips abroad, however, are supposed to ensure they are only used for approved purposes.

sentences.en: President Joe Biden’s Department of Commerce proposed federal know-your-customer rules for data centers in 2024, but no further progress appears to have been made under Donald Trump.
sentences.zh: 乔·拜登总统领导的商务部提议在 2024 年为数据中心制定联邦“了解你的客户”规则，但在唐纳德·特朗普的领导下似乎没有取得进一步进展。

sentences.en: Exporters shipping advanced chips abroad, however, are supposed to ensure they are only used for approved purposes.
sentences.zh: 然而，向国外运送先进芯片的出口商应该确保它们仅用于批准的目的。

blocks.html_en: Tim Fernholz is a journalist who writes about technology, finance and public policy. He has closely covered the rise of the private space industry and is the author of Rocket Billionaires: Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos and the New Space Race. Formerly, he was a senior reporter at Quartz, the global business news site, for more than a decade, and began his career as a political reporter in Washington, D.C. You can contact or verify outreach from Tim by emailing tim.fernholz@techcrunch.com or via an encrypted message to tim_fernholz.21 on Signal.

content_html_zh:
白宫科学顾问 Michael Kratsios 表示，Moonshot 是最大的开放式法学硕士 Kimi K3 背后的中国公司，它通过复制 Anthropic 的 Fable LLM 建立模型，同时使用未获准出口到中国的芯片。

Kratsios 写道：“旨在窃取美国专有技术和破坏美国研究的大规模、隐蔽的工业蒸馏是不可接受的。”据报道，正在讨论禁止中国开放权重模型，这些模型扰乱了人工智能行业。 Moonshot 没有回应有关其培训过程的问题，Kratsios 也没有透露有关其指控来源的更多细节。

Kratsios 的推文呼应了财政部长 Scott Bessent 的评论，即“我们在许多中国模型上发现了美国大语言模型的水印，这是不可接受的。” 目前尚不清楚这些水印由什么组成，财政部也没有回应询问。

然而，专家们对蒸馏（查询法学硕士以确定其内部运作并复制其功能的过程）是否是 Kimi K3 所展示的高级功能的原因表示怀疑。

劳德研究所 (Laude Institute) 研究员、Snorkel AI 联合创始人布雷登·汉考克 (Braden Hancock) 告诉 TechCrunch：“我认为，在《神鬼寓言》严格蒸馏之后，你不会得到如此强大、如此迅速的模型。” “坦白说，根本就没有时间，对吧？ 《神鬼寓言》自 7 月 1 日起才向公众开放。 你不可能在两周内提取那么多数据、训练模型并发布它。”

艾伦人工智能研究所的人工智能研究员内森·兰伯特 (Nathan Lambert) 在昨天发布的播客中表示：“我一直认为，随着时间的推移，随着中国模型越来越接近前沿，并且训练制度转向[强化学习]，蒸馏的影响力越来越小。” “如果是这样的话，每个人都可以通过使用其数据进行蒸馏来轻松赶上 GLM 或 K3。 但我们还没有，或者说我们不会仅仅通过监督微调来看到这一点。”

执行蒸馏需要实验室系统地查询其目标模型，以便生成可用于后期训练的数据。 有时，这明确涉及要求模型阐明其思想链以了解其如何解决问题。 其他时候，模型的提示和响应用于在称为监督微调（SFT）的过程中训练新模型。

正是这种微调过程可能会导致表面上由第三方创建的模型声称是克劳德。 在兰伯特看来，微调是“模型恢复其行为方式”的地方。

但 Lambert 表示，随着模型变得越来越复杂，SFT 的好处变得越来越不重要。 要提炼类似寓言的能力可能需要强化学习技术。 在许多情况下，这意味着让较大模型的代理对较小模型的响应进行评分，并根据评分进行调整。

更先进的技术也需要更重要的基础设施。 大型强化学习运行可能需要数千万个代理。 使用前沿实验室的 API 来做到这一点“将非常昂贵，并且可能会成为时间瓶颈，因为这些模型非常慢，坦率地说，甚至可能不会给你带来性能提升。”

似乎以前的前沿模型可能对 Kimi 做出了贡献； Anthropic 今年早些时候公开指责 Moonshot、DeepSeek 和 MiniMax 系统地提炼其模型。 Anthropic 表示，它发现其模型与通过 IP 地址和其他元数据在这些公司识别的用户之间进行了数百万次交换。 这些查询“与正常的使用模式不同，反映了故意的能力提取，而不是合法的使用。” Anthropic 没有回应 TechCrunch 关于《寓言蒸馏》的询问。

然而，蒸馏在人工智能公司中被认为很常见，而不仅仅是在中国。 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 今年早些时候作证说，他的公司 SpaceXAI 提炼了 OpenAI 模型来开发 Grok，这种做法在业界很常见。 例如，蒸馏和开发合成数据集之间的界限可能相当模糊。

“总的来说，美国人低估了这些中国团队的技术专长，”汉考克说。 “Moonshot 的创始人之一是卡耐基梅隆大学的博士生。 这些都是合法的研究人员和工程师，正在做着扎实的工作。 ……如果美国模式陷入停滞，我认为中国的进步将会放缓，但仍将继续。 他们不仅仅是在这里搭便车。”

也很难从 Kratsios 评论的第二部分中分离出来——Moonshot 获得了先进的 Nvidia 芯片、Grace Blackwell 300，并且还访问了泰国配备 GB300 的服务器。 乔治城安全与新兴技术中心研究员萨姆·布雷斯尼克表示，这些芯片被禁止出口到中国，但黑市仍然存在。 今年5月，美国服务器制造商Supermicro的创始人因向中国走私先进芯片而被起诉。

“我支持了解全球数据中心的客户法律，”布雷斯尼克说。 “如果你让一家公司在你最先进的硬件上进行大规模的培训，就需要有一个报告机制来说明该公司是谁以及他们在做什么。”

乔·拜登总统领导的商务部提议在 2024 年为数据中心制定联邦“了解你的客户”规则，但在唐纳德·特朗普的领导下似乎没有取得进一步进展。 然而，向国外运送先进芯片的出口商应该确保它们仅用于批准的目的。

当您通过我们文章中的链接购买时，我们可能会赚取少量佣金。 这并不影响我们的编辑独立性。

蒂姆·费恩霍尔茨 (Tim Fernholz) 是一名记者，撰写有关技术、金融和公共政策的文章。 他密切关注私人太空产业的崛起，是《火箭亿万富翁：埃隆·马斯克、杰夫·贝佐斯和新太空竞赛》一书的作者。 此前，他曾在全球商业新闻网站 Quartz 担任高级记者十多年，并在华盛顿特区开始了他的政治记者职业生涯。 您可以通过发送电子邮件至 tim.fernholz@techcrunch.com 或通过 Signal 上的加密消息发送至 tim_fernholz.21 来联系或验证 Tim 的联系。
